MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jeffery P. Woo; Cooper White & Cooper LLP; 415-433-1900; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants and cross-complainants Helen Marie Scholten, individually and as Trustee of the 2003 Scholten Family Trust U/D/T December 4, 2003, and Lisabeth Scholten Finci (erroneously sued as Elizabeth Scholten).
Jak Marquez; Dowling & Marquez LLP; 415-977-0444 x 232; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff and cross-defendant Mark Lambrose Kurzenhauser a/k/a Marc Lambros.

Defendants contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS HELEN SCHOLTEN AND LISABETH FINCI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION:

Motion for summary adjudication as to the cause of action for conversion is denied as moot; dismissal of that cause of action on file. Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is framed by the pleadings. Plaintiff concedes its burden to plead and prove an existence of a contract. Operative complaint only alleges an oral partnership agreement formed in 2001 by Plaintiff, Blake Scholten and James Scholten, and as original trustor (no allegation in the complaint as to the ownership of the property at the time of the agreement or if Mr. Scholten was also the sole original trustee), under which James Scholten stopped performing in 2002; the agreement was later amended as described in paragraph 8 (the Court will note that no deadlines for performance under the contract, including transfer of interest or payment is alleged) and the only parties to that amended agreement were Plaintiff and james Scholten. There are no allegations in the complaint showing how Helen Scholten in her capacity as a trustee or individually became a party to this contract. Opposition to the motion for summary judgment suggests that Plaintiff may be asserting some kind of ratification, succession (either as individual or as trustee) or formation of a separate agreement with Helen Scholten by conduct (either as individual or as trustee). Therefore, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege facts showing that Helen Scholten, as individual or trustee, is a party to the 2004 amended partnership agreement as described in paragraph 8 of the complaint or some other partnership agreement formed between 2004 and 2017. Cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty depends on the existence of the partnership agreement to which Helen Scholten is a party and Plaintiff shall have leave to include this cause of action in the amended complaint only if Plaintiff can in good faith allege facts to establish existence of the agreement. The Court does not reach the issue of the statute of limitations at this time.
Cause of action for Interference with a contract is predicated on existence of a valid contract. Plaintiff alleges a conclusion that a valid contract existed. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing that Plaintiff had a right under recorded title or agreement (e.g. lease, partnership agreement, management agreement, employment agreement) to occupy and rent the premises to third parties. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege such facts, how and when title was recorded; how and when the agreement was formed (note paragraph 8 of complaint does not allege right to possess and rent independent from agreement to transfer ownership interest); who were the parties to the agreement, what were the terms of the agreement, etc.
Trial date is vacated. Case is re-set to CMC calendar. Plaintiff shall have 10 days leave to amend.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
